headline: Chest-high S windswell but the angle's wrong and the wind's on it — junky.
today: There's size out there, 3-4ft, but it's short-period S swell hitting from a marginal angle so it'll be mushy and closed-out. Wind never really cooperates — cross to cross-on all day, going onshore by evening. If you go, the WSW cross around mid-morning near the low is your least-bad window; grab the fish and keep expectations low.

daysToWatch.note: Cleanest of the stretch: 2.5-3ft at a fair angle with N offshore dawn through morning. Get there early before the afternoon cross-on fills in — groveler or fish.
Small drop to 1.5-2ft but SE good angle and glassy 3mph dawn. Log the early window; wind swings onshore by afternoon.
